sources.name: legislation.gov.uk
sources.coverage: UK primary + secondary legislation (UKPGA, UKSI, ASP, WSI, NISI); ~150k items; 'as enacted' + revised/point-in-time versions; comprehensive from 1988+
sources.access: RESTful Legislation API with URI-based content negotiation; Atom feeds; bulk via data.gov.uk — exact bulk-dump mechanism uncertain — verify
sources.format: CLML XML (native), Akoma Ntoso XML (/data.akn), HTML5, PDF, plaintext
sources.license: Open Government Licence v3.0 (Crown copyright) — commercial + derivative reuse allowed with attribution

sources.name: Find Case Law (The National Archives)
sources.coverage: Judgments from E&W courts + UK-wide UKSC + Privy Council (since 2009 for UKSC); launched April 2022; excludes Scotland, NI, Ireland
sources.access: Public REST API + Atom feed; docs at nationalarchives.github.io/ds-find-caselaw-docs/public
sources.license: Open Justice Licence. CRITICAL: computational analysis (i.e., RAG ingestion) requires a separate free licence — apply to caselawlicence@nationalarchives.gov.uk. 'External indexing of documents' not permitted under default licence.

sources.name: BAILII
sources.coverage: ~411k cases + ~150k legislation items; UK (incl. Scotland, NI), Ireland, EU, Commonwealth; some historical back to 13th c.
sources.access: HTML only; robots.txt DISALLOWS crawlers; ToS explicitly prohibit bulk download and external indexing
sources.license: Mixed — underlying content Crown/Irish/EU copyright; BAILII HTML enrichment is BAILII's copyright. SCRAPING-RISKY — do not bulk-ingest.

sources.name: UK Supreme Court
sources.access: Direct PDF downloads; no documented structured API; mirrored on Find Case Law
sources.license: Crown copyright; OGL-eligible per judiciary guidance (per-item OGL badge uncertain — verify)

sources.name: Judiciary.uk
sources.coverage: Sentencing remarks, selected judgments, speeches, practice directions (E&W)
sources.access: HTML/PDF; no API; judgments increasingly redirect to Find Case Law
sources.license: Crown copyright / OGL for most; some speeches may carry author copyright

sources.name: UK Parliament APIs (Hansard, Bills)
sources.coverage: Hansard (both Houses, historic 1803–2005 at /historic-hansard), Bills, Members, Divisions, Committees, Treaties, Statutory Instruments
sources.access: Versioned REST; SPARQL endpoint for Treaties + SIs; rate limits uncertain — verify
